meta_title: Impact Windows in Three Islands, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs HVHZ-rated impact windows in Three Islands, FL. ESW & CWI brands, in-house crews, A+ BBB. Financing from $87/mo. Call for a free quote!
h1: Impact Windows Built for Three Islands Living — Storm-Ready, Code-Compliant, and Built to Last
intro:
Three Islands is one of Broward County's most distinctive residential communities — a neighborhood built around water, privacy, and elevated standards. Nestled within ZIP code 33009 and bordered by Hallandale Beach, Hollywood, and Pembroke Park, Three Islands attracts homeowners who invest in their properties and expect the same quality in every upgrade they make. With an average home value hovering around $500,000, this is a community that understands the relationship between smart home improvements and lasting financial returns. Impact windows are one of the single most impactful investments Three Islands homeowners can make — and Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, South Florida's Kings of Service, is the team trusted to deliver them right.

Despite sitting roughly three miles from the coastline, Three Islands is far from immune to the destructive forces that South Florida storms routinely unleash. The community falls squarely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ), the most demanding wind-resistance designation in the entire state. Broward County as a whole has been struck by more than ten named storms since 1990, and neighborhoods like Three Islands have felt the full brunt of events like Hurricane Andrew in 1992, Hurricane Wilma in 2005, and Hurricane Irma in 2017. Standard windows — even so-called "hurricane-resistant" ones that don't meet HVHZ specifications — simply aren't engineered to handle what South Florida throws at them season after season.

Approximately 35% of homes in the Three Islands area were built before 1994, meaning they predate the sweeping code reforms that followed the catastrophic damage of Hurricane Andrew. Many of these homes still have single-pane or older aluminum-frame windows that offer virtually no meaningful protection against wind-borne debris or sustained storm pressure. Even newer homes in the area benefit enormously from upgrading to certified HVHZ-rated impact windows, which deliver year-round protection — not just during storms — by improving energy efficiency, reducing outside noise from nearby traffic along US-1 and I-95, and dramatically boosting curb appeal and resale value in an already competitive Broward market.

section_why.h2: Why Three Islands Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When you're protecting a home worth $500,000 or more in a High Velocity Hurricane Zone, the contractor you choose matters as much as the products they install. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties on one core principle: do the work right, with our own people, every single time. That means no subcontractors, no third-party crews, and no shortcuts. Every impact window installation in Three Islands is performed by our in-house team of certified installers who carry 60+ years of combined experience in South Florida's demanding construction environment.

With over 500 completed homes across Broward County and beyond, our crews have worked on everything from pre-Andrew ranch-style homes in established neighborhoods to modern Mediterranean villas along the waterways. We understand the specific structural quirks, building envelope challenges, and permit requirements that come with homes in Three Islands. Our installations are not copy-paste jobs — we measure, assess, and custom-fit every window to the exact specifications of each home, ensuring a watertight, code-compliant finish that holds up for decades.

Premier Impact Windows & Roofing carries an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, and that rating reflects thousands of interactions with homeowners who expected honesty, transparency, and exceptional craftsmanship. We walk every client through the product selection process, explain the Florida Building Code requirements that apply to their specific property, and handle all permitting through the Broward County Building Division so homeowners never have to navigate that process alone. From your first free estimate to the final inspection sign-off, our team is with you every step of the way.

Our clients in Three Islands and surrounding communities like Hallandale Beach and Hollywood choose us because they know that impact window installation is not a place to cut corners. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is fully licensed and insured in the state of Florida, and we stand behind our work with the confidence that comes from experience, integrity, and a genuine commitment to protecting the homes and families we serve.

section_history.h2: Storm History That Shaped Three Islands — And Why Protection Is Non-Negotiable
section_history.body:
Three Islands may be a quiet, upscale enclave by South Florida standards, but its history is written in part by the storms that have repeatedly tested this region's homes, businesses, and infrastructure. Broward County has experienced more than ten named storms since 1990, and the area's geography — positioned between the Atlantic coast and the inland flatlands of South Florida — means that wind damage, storm surge effects, and wind-borne debris impacts are real threats every single hurricane season.

Hurricane Andrew in 1992 remains the defining storm event in South Florida's modern history. Though its most catastrophic damage was concentrated in Miami-Dade County, the storm's outer bands hammered Broward communities and exposed the alarming inadequacy of the construction standards of the era. It was Andrew that ultimately forced the state of Florida to overhaul its building codes and establish the HVHZ designation that governs communities like Three Islands today. Homes built before 1994 — and roughly 35% of Three Islands properties fall into that category — were constructed under standards we now know to be dangerously insufficient.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 and Hurricane Irma in 2017 further reinforced this lesson, causing widespread window failures, roof damage, and interior flooding across Broward County, even in homes that had been updated over the years. Wilma, in particular, was notable for its rapid intensification and the sheer scope of damage it inflicted on communities from Miami-Dade through Palm Beach County. For Three Islands homeowners who still have older windows — or even newer windows that weren't installed to HVHZ standards — each hurricane season represents a gamble that Premier Impact Windows & Roofing can help eliminate permanently.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Building Codes and Permit Requirements for Impact Windows in Three Islands
section_code.body:
Three Islands falls within the High Velocity Hurricane Zone, which means impact window installations here are held to the strictest building code standards in the state of Florida — and arguably among the most rigorous in the entire country. The Florida Building Code (FBC) mandates that all windows and glazing products installed in HVHZ communities must pass both the Large Missile Impact Test and the Cyclic Wind Pressure Test, as defined by Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or the Florida Product Approval process. These aren't marketing designations — they are legally enforceable requirements that every product and installation must satisfy before a permit can be approved.

All impact window installations in Three Islands require permits through the Broward County Building Division. This process includes submitting product approval documentation, engineered drawings where applicable, and installation specifications that demonstrate full compliance with the current Florida Building Code. Following installation, a licensed inspector must review and approve the work before the permit can be closed. This isn't bureaucratic red tape — it's the system that ensures every home in the HVHZ is genuinely protected by products and workmanship that have been independently verified to meet the standard.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we manage the entire permitting process on behalf of our Three Islands clients. Our team is intimately familiar with Broward County Building Division requirements, and we ensure that all ESW and CWI impact window products we install carry the appropriate Miami-Dade NOA or Florida Product Approval designations required for HVHZ compliance. When our work is done, homeowners receive a fully permitted, inspected, and code-compliant installation — protecting not just their home, but their insurance coverage, resale value, and legal standing as well.

section_products.h2: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI Impact Windows: Premium Protection for Three Islands Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most trusted impact window brands available in South Florida: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both product lines are specifically engineered and tested to meet HVHZ requirements, making them ideal for Three Islands homeowners who need real, code-compliant protection rather than products that simply claim to be storm-resistant. Selecting the right brand and product line for your home depends on your architectural style, budget priorities, and performance expectations — and our team helps every client make that decision with confidence.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) offers a premium impact window system built around heavy-duty multi-chamber frames that dramatically reduce heat transfer, air infiltration, and outside noise. ESW windows feature laminated safety glass with Low-E coatings that block a significant portion of the sun's infrared and ultraviolet energy — a critical benefit for Three Islands homeowners who deal with intense South Florida sun year-round. The Low-E glass helps keep interior spaces cooler, reduces the load on air conditioning systems, and protects interior furnishings and flooring from UV fading. ESW products are engineered to withstand the high wind pressures and impact forces required under HVHZ certification, offering genuine protection when storms make landfall.

CWI impact windows provide an equally robust solution with a strong track record of performance in South Florida's demanding climate. Known for their clean sightlines, smooth operation, and excellent structural integrity, CWI windows are a popular choice for homeowners who want high performance without sacrificing aesthetics. Both ESW and CWI products are available in a range of styles — including single-hung, double-hung, casement, picture, and sliding configurations — ensuring that Three Islands homeowners can protect their homes without compromising the architectural character that makes their properties distinctive and valuable.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Three Islands — And How to Finance Them Smartly
section_cost.body:
Impact window costs in Three Islands vary depending on the number of openings, window styles, frame material, glass package, and whether the existing openings require any structural modification prior to installation. For a typical Three Islands home with an average value around $500,000, homeowners can generally expect to invest somewhere in the range of $12,000 to $30,000 for a whole-home impact window replacement, though smaller projects covering key exposures can come in below that range. Every home is different, which is why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides detailed, no-obligation estimates tailored to each property — not ballpark numbers that leave clients with budget surprises later.

The return on that investment is substantial and measurable. Impact windows consistently rank among the highest-ROI home improvements in South Florida's real estate market. In a community like Three Islands where homes are valued at roughly $500,000, buyers actively look for properties that are already equipped with impact protection — it's a meaningful selling point that can accelerate a sale and support a higher asking price. Beyond resale value, the energy savings delivered by Low-E laminated glass can reduce monthly cooling costs noticeably, and insurance premium reductions can amount to hundreds — sometimes thousands — of dollars per year.

For homeowners who want to move forward without a large upfront payment, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified applicants. Financing makes it possible for Three Islands homeowners to protect their investment and start benefiting from lower insurance premiums and energy bills immediately, while spreading the cost comfortably over time. Our team works with you to identify the financing structure that best fits your budget and timeline.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings and Wind Mitigation Credits for Three Islands Impact Window Owners
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial arguments for installing impact windows in Three Islands is the direct effect they have on homeowner's insurance premiums. Florida's insurance market — particularly in Broward County's HVHZ — is among the most expensive in the nation, driven by the region's well-documented vulnerability to hurricane damage. Insurers have responded to this risk environment by offering meaningful discounts to policyholders who take proactive steps to harden their homes against storm damage. Installing HVHZ-certified impact windows throughout your home is one of the most significant steps you can take.

Under Florida law, homeowners are entitled to request a wind mitigation inspection after impact windows are installed. A licensed inspector evaluates the home's opening protection, roof connections, and other structural features, then produces a wind mitigation report that is submitted to your insurance carrier. Most major insurers operating in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — provide substantial premium credits for homes with full impact window protection on all openings. These credits can reduce your annual wind premium by 20% to 45% or more, depending on your carrier, policy structure, and the specific features of your home.

For a Three Islands homeowner paying $6,000 to $10,000 or more annually in homeowner's insurance — not uncommon in this area given elevated home values and HVHZ exposure — an insurance reduction of even 25% represents $1,500 to $2,500 in annual savings. Compounded over time, those savings meaningfully offset the cost of the impact window investment itself. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing helps clients understand how to initiate the wind mitigation inspection process and what documentation their insurance carrier will need to apply the available credits.

faqs.q: Do I really need HVHZ-rated impact windows if Three Islands is a few miles from the coast?
faqs.a: Yes, absolutely. Three Islands falls within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone regardless of its distance from the shoreline. HVHZ designation is determined by wind speed maps and regional storm exposure data, not just coastal proximity. Broward County has experienced 10+ named storms since 1990, and inland neighborhoods experience the same destructive wind pressures that coastal communities face during major hurricanes. HVHZ-rated impact windows are legally required for any new installation or replacement in Three Islands.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical home in Three Islands, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For most Three Islands homes, a complete impact window replacement typically ranges from $12,000 to $30,000 depending on the number of openings, window styles, and glass packages selected. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified buyers. When you factor in insurance savings and energy efficiency gains, many homeowners find that impact windows pay for themselves significantly faster than expected.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my windows with impact windows in Three Islands?
faqs.a: Yes. All impact window installations in Three Islands require a permit issued through the Broward County Building Division. Products must carry Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or Florida Product Approval certifications that comply with HVHZ standards. A licensed inspector must review and approve the installation before the permit can be closed. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf, from application through final inspection sign-off.

faqs.q: How long does the impact window installation process take for a Three Islands home?
faqs.a: The typical timeline for a complete impact window installation in Three Islands runs from four to eight weeks from signed contract to completed installation, accounting for permit approval, product fabrication, and scheduling. The physical installation itself usually takes one to three days depending on the size of the home and number of openings. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing coordinates every phase of the process and keeps homeowners informed throughout so there are no surprises.

faqs.q: How much can I save on my homeowner's insurance after installing impact windows in Three Islands?
faqs.a: Three Islands homeowners who install HVHZ-certified impact windows on all openings and complete a wind mitigation inspection typically qualify for significant premium reductions from their insurance carrier. Savings commonly range from 20% to 45% or more on the wind portion of the policy, depending on the carrier and coverage structure. For homeowners in Broward County paying $6,000 to $10,000+ annually in premiums, this can translate to $1,500 to $4,000 or more in yearly savings — making impact windows one of the smartest financial investments available.

cta_body: Contact Premier Impact Windows & Roofing today for your free, no-obligation estimate — our in-house team is ready to bring HVHZ-certified impact window protection to your Three Islands home with the craftsmanship and service that have made us South Florida's Kings of Service.
